message:
feat(traceback): add syntax highlighting and styled rendering

Transforms traceback output from plain text to richly styled segments with
semantic coloring that matches common IDE/terminal conventions.

Style assignments:
- File paths: cyan - standard convention for paths/URIs
- Line numbers: yellow - draws attention to location
- Function names: green - distinguishes callable context
- Dim style: separators and connectors (":","in")
- Error line indicator (❱): bold red for the failing line
- Exception type: bold red for emphasis
- Exception message: red for error context

Rendering changes:
- Frame headers now emit multiple styled Segment objects instead of
  a single formatted string
- Source context lines use conditional styling (error line vs context)
- Panel border now styled red to match error theme
- Exception info split into separately styled type and message segments

Implementation details:
- Styles parsed once at render start, cloned per-use
- Fallback to None style if parse fails (graceful degradation)
- Line indicator "❱" vs " " for error vs context lines
- Preserved existing width/layout calculations

This brings traceback rendering closer to Python Rich's colored output
while maintaining compatibility with non-color terminals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
